free_text: Neutron scattering by U-235 and Pu-239 for incident
6 MV Van de Graaff, terminal pulsing and
The incident energies, 2, 3 and 4 MeV are nominal but
are correct within the limits of the overall neutron
energy spreads which are about +- 50 keV.
The samples were hollow cylinders. nominally
2 in. high, 1 in. outer diam. and 0.65 in. inner
diam., and were canned in aluminium of total wall
flight path = 1.74 m. The resolution of the
by interpolating each continuous non-elastic spectrum
underneath the elastic 'peak' and subtracting to give
counts in the peak. The peak counts represent
only true elastic.(If the excitation of low lying
levels is not enhanced by direct interactions.)
The continuous non-elastic time spectra are converted
to energy spectra in histogram form, each energy band
corresponding roughly to one resolution width.
Cross sections were calculated on the assumption that
the samples consisted of one isotope only. This is not
expected to lead to a large error since the (n,n')
cross section and spectra of various isotopes are not
A polythene sample was used to
relate cross sections to the hydrogen scattering cross
The secondary energy spectra for U-235 and Pu-239 have
been analysed into fission and inelastic components,
the analysis showing possible evidence that the
fission component is a little harder than anticipated.
Because of this uncertainty about the fission
component, the shape of the inelastic component is not
In calculating cross sections, corrections were made
The errors on the spectra are about 5% and
* errors in the knowledge of the backgrounds,
* errors in the detector efficiency calibration and
At the very low energies, in the region below about
600 keV, the errors on the individual steps exceed
5% and are not easy to determine precisely.

The assumption that the shape of the (n,n') cross-
section is given by n(E) = B * exp(-E/t) was used.
For, multiple scattering in the samples by an
extension of the method by Cranberg (see REL-REF)
Inelastic neutron spectra were obtained by subtraction
of the fission spectra from the non-elastic spectra.
The fission spectra are represented by
data taken from table 3 of AWRE-O-55/69.
